title: Work with Iframes
description: Learn to locate and interact with elements inside iframes
instructions:
Write a Playwright test that:
1. Navigates to a page with iframes
2. Locates an iframe
3. Accesses elements inside the iframe
4. Performs actions on iframe content
5. Verifies the interactions

starterCode.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_work_with_iframes(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://practice.expandtesting.com/iframe')
    
    # TODO: Locate the iframe
    
    # TODO: Get frame locator
    
    # TODO: Interact with elements inside iframe
    
    # TODO: Verify content
    pass

solution.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_work_with_iframes(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://practice.expandtesting.com/iframe')
    
    frame = page.frame_locator('#mce_0_ifr')
    editor = frame.locator('#tinymce')
    
    editor.clear()
    editor.fill('Hello from Playwright!')
    
    expect(editor).to_contain_text('Hello from Playwright!')
    
    # Alternative: using page.frame()
    frame_element = page.frame(url=re.compile('.*iframe.*'))
    if frame_element:
        content = frame_element.locator('#tinymce').text_content()
        assert 'Hello' in content

hints: Use page.frame() to get frame handle
frameLocator chains just like regular locators
No need to switch context explicitly
Can locate iframes by selector, name, or URL

testCases.name: Iframe interaction works
testCases.expectedBehavior: Text is entered in iframe editor
